Discovery: Down ~67% from multi-year highs with shares at ~$4; Q1 2026 revenue +12% YoY to $1.53B and Adjusted EBITDA surged to $233M vs $108M prior year — the profitability turn is real and happening now; North America advertiser upfront commitments +10% YoY signals agency confidence; Q2 2026 earnings (July 2026) is the next catalyst. Bear case: GAAP unprofitable, regulatory risk (children online safety), user attrition in key geographies, stock trades at only 7.5x 2026E vs Meta at 24x (discount may be justified). Goldman target $9.25 / Morgan Stanley $8.50 imply 2x upside from current levels.
value_picks.analyst_take: Social media platform at multi-year lows with AR glasses hardware + AI-powered Snap Lens monetization pipeline providing optionality; digital ad recovery + Snapchat+ subscription driving toward FCF breakeven

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: AVLAYAH (tividenofusp alfa) FDA accelerated approval confirmed March 25, 2026 — correcting context file which listed outdated July 2026 PDUFA date; commercial launch in progress; wait for Q2 2026 sales data before entering (Tier 2 company PR)
Discovery: PDUFA date July 7, 2026 for DNL310 (rare disease/Hunter syndrome); $1.1B market cap binary event with significant upside on approval in an underserved rare disease space.
growth_picks.analyst_take: AVLAYAH received FDA accelerated approval March 25, 2026 for Hunter syndrome — binary event already resolved positively; now a commercialization ramp story; context file's July 2026 PDUFA date was outdated

avoid_picks.analyst_take: Trading at 100x+ forward earnings after Falcon platform outage reputational damage; enterprise customers diversifying endpoint security; growth decelerating; risk/reward deeply unfavorable at current multiple
AI ASIC designer without proven at-scale customer adoption; pre-meaningful-revenue with extreme valuation; competitive pressure from Broadcom/Marvell in custom silicon; speculative not investable

Cloudflare at 30x forward revenue is expensive for decelerating growth; SASE/Zero Trust market commoditizing rapidly; Zscaler and Palo Alto competition intensifying in core security segments; not cheap enough for quality

CPaaS market structurally compressing margins as mobile carriers build direct API offerings; AI-native communication platforms are existential medium-term threat; no defensible moat at current valuation

86% short float on IPO-lockup structure creates binary risk; force majeure arbitration with Repsol/Shell could result in massive penalty payments; litigation overhang makes risk/reward unattractive despite fundamental EBITDA improvement

Cable operator facing secular cord-cutting + fixed wireless broadband substitution from T-Mobile/Verizon; $95B+ debt limits strategic optionality; fiber-to-the-home competition from AT&T/Frontier accelerating in footprint

DOJ antitrust investigation into Optum vertical integration; Brian Thompson assassination political/regulatory scrutiny fallout ongoing; Medicare Advantage reimbursement pressure continuing; multiple binary regulatory risks not quantifiable

Bitcoin mining operator with no differentiation; hash rate difficulty rising faster than BTC price; no credible AI/HPC transition track record; speculative instrument not suitable as a thesis-driven investment

After 93% one-month rally to $1,064 the easy money is made; old analyst targets ($550) stale and new targets range $550-$1,750 showing extreme uncertainty; wait for post-June-24-earnings dip to sub-$900 before entering
